title: Utilizing Microbes for Human Benefit
author: Sayan Bhattacharyya *
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This review highlights the beneficial applications of microorganisms in improving human health and quality of life. It explores the roles of microbes in producing essential vitamins, strengthening the immune system, and serving as probiotics that combat pathogens in the gut. The study also discusses their use in medical and industrial fields, including the production of antibiotics, antifungal agents, therapeutic enzymes, and biodegradable materials. Additionally, microbes play critical roles in waste degradation, vaccine development, and molecular biology innovations like PCR. The research underscores the vast potential of microorganisms as a cornerstone for advancements in medicine, public health, and environmental sustainability.
